pr.title: Fix static/extern linkage issues in nonmatching files
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Ported static/extern linkage corrections from a src-only build branch to reduce diff noise and fix nonmatching-file linkage declarations across camera, effects, Yoshi, fighter device, game mode, ground, library, menu, and baselib code. The PR mainly changed whether symbols are file-local static definitions, external definitions, or public declarations, and moved several BSS/data definitions into the owning C files rather than leaving misleading extern declarations in local scopes or headers.
what_changed: Changed duplicate helper functions named vec_len in camera.c and ground.c from global functions to static inline helpers, avoiding unintended external linkage while preserving local inline matching behavior.

Made several data objects non-static when another translation unit already references them, including efAsync_803C025C in efasync.c, efLib_80458EE0 in eflib.c, gm_804D67EC and gm_804D67F0 in gm_1A4C.c, ftYs_Unk3_803CED48 in ftYs_SpecialN.static.h, and mn_804D6BB8 / mn_804D6BC8 in mnmain.c.

Changed Yoshi special-N/S-related function prototypes in ftYs_SpecialN.h and ftYs_SpecialN.static.h from static declarations to normal external declarations for fn_8012CEE0, fn_8012CF7C, ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF00, fn_8012E644, and ftYs_SpecialS_8012EAD8.
Changed ground.c prototypes for Ground_801C20E0, Ground_801C28CC, Ground_801C34AC, and Ground_801C466C from static to externally linked declarations.

Converted several local extern declarations that actually describe file-local storage into static definitions, including gm_804D6850, gm_8049E548, gm_804D6900, gm_804D6730, lbDvd_803BA68C, mnGallery_804D6C8C, HSD_SisLib_804D796C, and multiple mnmain menu globals.
Added ftdevice.c definitions for ft_80459A68, ft_804D6570, ft_804D6578, and ftDevice_BuryThingCount, while removing ft_804D6570 from ftdevice.h because it is now file-local static.
Removed HSD_SisLib_804D7968 and HSD_SisLib_804D796C extern declarations from sislib.h and instead added file-local definitions in sislib.c.

In mnmain.c, consolidated scattered local extern declarations near use sites into a single block of top-level definitions for menu globals such as mn_804D6BA8, mn_804D6BAC, mn_804D6BB0, mn_804D6BB4, mn_804D6BB5, MenMain_lights, MenMain_fog, and MenMain_cam.
smart_moves: Treated linkage as part of the decomp source model rather than as harmless declaration noise: symbols with true file-local ownership were made static, while symbols referenced by other files were given external definitions or declarations.
Used static inline for same-named local helpers such as vec_len, which avoids global symbol collisions and keeps the helper available for matching-sensitive local code generation.
Moved file-owned BSS/data definitions into the corresponding C files instead of leaving them as extern declarations in headers, reducing false public API surface.
Kept address comments on prototypes and data declarations while changing only linkage qualifiers, preserving the searchable decomp annotation style.
Cleaned scattered in-function or mid-file extern declarations by replacing them with top-level definitions, making ownership clearer for future matching and src-only builds.
decomp_lessons: In nonmatching files, incorrect static versus extern qualifiers can create build/linkage problems even when functions are not yet fully matched.

A declaration in a .static.h should not automatically be static; if the symbol is shared with another translation unit or represents a real externally linked function/data symbol, use a non-static declaration.
Headers should expose only symbols that are genuinely external. File-local BSS/data should live as static definitions in the owning .c file rather than as externs in public headers.
If two files define helper functions with the same C name, mark them static or static inline unless the original binary requires one exported symbol.
Porting fixes from a stricter src-only build can reveal linkage inconsistencies that are hidden or tolerated in the normal decomp workflow.

When converting extern declarations to definitions, check whether the object should be initialized data, BSS, SDATA, or a static local symbol; this PR mostly corrected linkage, not names or structure layouts.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-derived placeholder names such as gm_804D67EC, ft_80459A68, mn_804D6BB8, HSD_SisLib_804D7968, and efAsync_803C025C when the purpose was linkage cleanup rather than semantic renaming.
Kept subsystem prefixes consistent with file ownership: Camera_, efAsync_, efLib_, ftDevice_, ftYs_, gm_, Ground_, lbDvd_, mnGallery_, mn_, MenMain_, and HSD_SisLib_.
Retained address comments in the form /* 12CEE0 */ or /* 4D6574 */ next to declarations, which keeps linker/map correspondence visible after linkage edits.
Used static.h files for internal/nonpublic declarations, but this PR shows that static.h contents may still contain non-static declarations when the function or object has external linkage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use static inline for small local helpers like vec_len to avoid creating extra externally visible symbols and to encourage inlining patterns needed by matching code.
Correct storage duration/linkage before attempting deeper matching, because wrong extern/static qualifiers can change emitted symbols, duplicate definitions, or unresolved references.
For BSS/data symbols, prefer one real definition in the owning C file plus extern declarations only where cross-file references require them.
When a nonmatching C file temporarily needs declarations for functions defined later or elsewhere, make the prototype linkage match the actual symbol rather than marking everything static by default.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the motivation: porting changes from a src-only build branch to simplify the diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove TODOs or move ftYs_SpecialN declarations from headers into the C file.
Search for remaining local extern declarations in nonmatching C files that should be static definitions.
Audit .static.h files for symbols declared static despite being referenced across translation units.
Look for duplicate helper names like vec_len that should be static inline to avoid global linkage conflicts.
Check whether src-only build branch linkage fixes were fully ported for other subsystems not touched in PR 1772.
